back_translation: The men of Israel had sworn at Mizpah, saying, 'No man of us will give his daughter to Benjamin as a wife.'
The people came to Bethel, and they sat there until evening before God, and they lifted up their voice and wept a great weeping.
They said, 'Why, O LORD God of Israel, has this happened in Israel — that today one tribe should be missing from Israel?'
It was on the next day that the people rose early and built there an altar and offered up burnt offerings and peace offerings.

The sons of Israel said, 'Who is the one who did not come up in the assembly from all the tribes of Israel to the LORD?' For the great oath had been sworn against the one who did not come up to the LORD at Mizpah, saying, 'He shall surely be put to death.'
The sons of Israel were grieved over Benjamin their brother, and they said, 'Today one tribe has been cut off from Israel.'
'What shall we do for them — for those who remain — for wives, when we ourselves have sworn by the LORD not to give them of our daughters as wives?'
They said, 'Which one from the tribes of Israel did not come up to the LORD at Mizpah?' And behold, no man had come to the camp from Jabesh-gilead, to the assembly.
The people were mustered, and behold, there was not there a man from the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead.

The congregation sent there 12,000 men of the sons of valor, and they commanded them, saying, 'Go and strike the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead with the edge of the sword, and the women and the children.'
'And this is what you shall do: every male, and every woman knowing the lying of a male, you shall devote to destruction.'
They found from the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead 400 young virgin women who had not known a man in the lying of a male, and they brought them to the camp at Shiloh, which is in the land of Canaan.
The whole congregation sent and spoke to the sons of Benjamin who were at the rock of Rimmon, and they called peace to them.
Benjamin returned at that time, and they gave to them the women whom they had kept alive from the women of Jabesh-gilead, but they did not find enough for them so.
The people were grieved over Benjamin, because the LORD had made a breach in the tribes of Israel.
The elders of the congregation said, 'What shall we do for those who remain for wives, since woman has been destroyed from Benjamin?'
They said, 'An inheritance of escape belongs to Benjamin, that a tribe may not be wiped out from Israel.'
'But we are not able to give to them wives from our daughters, because the sons of Israel swore, saying, Cursed is the one who gives a wife to Benjamin.'

They said, 'Behold, a feast of the LORD is in Shiloh from days to days, which is from the north of Bethel, from the rising of the sun of the road that goes up from Bethel to Shechem, and from the south of Lebonah.'
They commanded the sons of Benjamin, saying, 'Go, and you shall lie in ambush in the vineyards.'

'And you shall see, and behold, if the daughters of Shiloh come out to dance in the dances, you shall come out from the vineyards, and you shall seize for yourselves each man his wife from the daughters of Shiloh, and go to the land of Benjamin.'

'And it shall be when their fathers or their brothers come to complain to us, we shall say to them, Give to us them, because we did not receive each man a wife in the war, because you did not give to them — it is a time you would be guilty.'

The sons of Benjamin did so, and they took up wives according to their number, from the dancing women whom they seized, and they went and returned to their inheritance, and they built the cities and dwelt in them.
The sons of Israel walked from there at that time, each man to his tribe and to his clan, and each man went out from there to his inheritance.
In those days there was no king in Israel; each man what was right in his own eyes he would do.
